title: Named pipes not being created in Windows when podman machine is started as a user that is not in administrators group
body:
### Issue Description

This Windows 11 system is security hardened and locked down by a restrictive GPO.
I've tried installing for all users and 'just me'.  In both cases, if I run as a user with admin privileges (but not running with elevated privileges) when the machine starts, \.\pipe\podman-machine-default is created and I can pull images, etc. from the Podman Desktop UI and the machine shell. When I run as a non-admin user, the named pipe is not created and pulling images shows a ENOENT error for \.\pipe\podman-machine-default. Running podman commands from the machine shell works without problem.
I'm using WSL2.
I've done a fresh install on a non-hardened Win11 system and everything works as expected.

I'm sure the problem is in the hardening, but I'm hoping for a clue as to what setting needs to be relaxed or whether there might be a work-around.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Probably specific to Windows machines hardened according to DISA STIG.
Installed using podman desktop airgap install as either 'just me' or 'all users'
Start the podman machine either as podman machine start from cli or from within the Desktop UI

### Describe the results you received

\\.\pipe\podman-machine-default and \\.\pipe\docker_engine not created.  All podman commands work as expected from within the podman machine shell.  I can login. pull, push.  But nothing works from Podman Desktop and ENOENT error for \\.\pipe\podman-machine-default is shown in logs.

### Describe the results you expected

\\.\pipe\podman-machine-default and \\.\pipe\docker_engine created and Podman Desktop is able to pull images, etc.

### podman info output

```yaml
From another system that isn't hardened but was installed from the same podman-desktop-airgap-1.25.1-setup-x64.exe package:
Client:
  APIVersion: 5.7.1
  Built: 1765378491
  BuiltTime: Wed Dec 10 09:54:51 2025
  GitCommit: f845d14e941889ba4c071f35233d09b29d363c75
  GoVersion: go1.25.5
  Os: windows
  OsArch: windows/amd64
  Version: 5.7.1
...
  databaseBackend: sqlite
  distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    variant: container
    version: "43"
...
  kernel: 6.6.87.2-microsoft-standard-WSL2
...
version:
  APIVersion: 5.7.1
  BuildOrigin: 'Copr: packit/containers-podman-27732'
  Built: 1765238400
  BuiltTime: Mon Dec  8 19:00:00 2025
  GitCommit: f845d14e941889ba4c071f35233d09b29d363c75
  GoVersion: go1.25.4 X:nodwarf5
  Os: linux
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 5.7.1
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

DISA STIG hardened Windows 11 system.

### Additional information

I know this is an unusual environment.  I'm just hoping for ideas on how to debug further or what might be preventing the named pipe creation.

comments.body:
@l0rd  thank you for those tips.  User-mode networking still failed, but running at debug log level shows me that win-sshproxy fails to start and the event logs are telling me that the permissions do not grant local activation permission for the COM server...
So, definitely some security setting is blocking.  I'll dig into the error messages a bit more to see if I can narrow it down
I created a new issue in the gvisor-tap-vsock repo for this.
Thank you @jfath. I am adding the link here for reference: https://github.com/containers/gvisor-tap-vsock/issues/629
